topic: Subagent context management patterns for multi-agent workflows

sources.title: Anthropic: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents
Martin Fowler: Context Engineering for Coding Agents
Maggie Appleton: Gas Town Agent Patterns
From Tasks to Swarms: Agent Teams
Team command (307 lines — largest context hog)
Implement command (202 lines — unconditional file reads)

summary: Context exhaustion is a known Claude Code bug (#18240, #17591) — cnogo's fat commands amplify it
Commands load 5-8K tokens of overhead before code reading begins (skills.md, agent tables, CONTEXT.md, PLAN.json)
Gas Town's transferable principle: persistent roles + ephemeral sessions; work state in git/SQLite, not context
Anthropic guidance: subagents should return 1-2K summaries; use JIT loading, not eager loading
Superpowers pattern: dispatch each task to fresh subagent with only needed context; persist progress to disk
System prompt budget: 2-5K tokens max; design tasks to complete within 50% context usage
Fix: inline skill refs, remove agent tables from team.md, add maxTurns, set early auto-compaction
recommendation: Combine three approaches: (A) inline skill references and remove dead-weight context from commands, (B) add maxTurns:30 and CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE=60 as safety nets, (C) make implementer task descriptions self-contained so subagents don't re-read source docs. Follow up by using memory engine as external coordination state.
